prompt:
Nightly shipment export: on nights when the upstream API blips once (transient 503-ish failure, our client surfaces it as TransientFetchError), the export is wrong in a very specific way — one record appears twice and another is missing entirely. I diffed Tuesday's run against the source: r-02 came through twice, and on a night where the blip hit the very first page request, the first record of the whole feed was just gone. Clean nights are byte-identical to the source. So the retry path in RecordPager is mangling the stream somehow.

Requirements the scripted fake-service tests pin down: every record yielded exactly once, in source order; cursors are opaque service tokens, so a retry re-requests exactly the cursor that failed; empty pages that carry a continuation cursor keep the stream going; max_retries is a per-page budget, and once it's exhausted the TransientFetchError propagates to the caller. `python3 test_pager.py` fails on the blip scenarios today. Fix pager.py; leave the tests alone.
